Letter from Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President, CONCERN Trust
Dear friends
We at CONCERN in the field of De-Addiction rehabilitation have been contributing to
the affected community and empowering change in many families since 2008. Since
inception we have adopted and followed simple, medical, psychological, holistic and
ethical standards.
The journey was arduous. Many a time the tests and trials were difficult to cross over. It
was more demanding since we did not deviate or compromise on moral, legal and
statutory obligations. We adopt only ethically prescribed practices of rehabilitation
treatment.
Our Mission, Vision and Values are altruistic. We stick to them and put it in practical
day to day functioning of CONCERN.
It is fulfilling and rewarding to note that CONCERN is rated high in success and in
quality by our clients who are in the path of recovery. We wish to remain grateful to all
who worked in tandem with us to enable CONCERN to accomplish this reward.
This Year 2024, we have entered a new venture. We have been recognized as “District
De-Addiction Center (DDAC)” by the “Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
(MJSE)” for the Scheme “Nation Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR).”
I thank the Higher Power who had chosen me in this Mission and I thank SBI, well-
wishers, donors, volunteers and specially MSJE. My special thanks to all my dedicated
staff who stand behind me to walk ahead to fulfill our Vision, Mission and Values.
Regards
Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President
CONCERN Trust

-- 2 of 67 --

Page | 3
Genesis
CONCERN is an NGO working in the field of addiction since 2008 Bhanu Suresh
babu, M.A., (M.Phil.) in psychology is the founder president of the Trust. She
underwent the perils of addiction in her family and found a way out by Divine
intervention, standing example of Dr. Shanthi Ranganathan, TTK Hospital and Al-
Anon fellowship.
Suresh Babu, M.B.A., C.A.I.I.B., D.C.A., Co-founder of CONCERN. He retired from
SBI as Manager. As a Secretary-Trustee, he manages the policy making, execution and
administration. His exposure to Addiction recovery tools is over 30 years.
Ms. Pavitra Ramachandran, M.Sc., in Bio Medical Genetics is the Treasurer of the
CONCERN Trust.
CONCERN started in a small Two-bedroom house in Valasaravakkam with Ten beds is
now a full-fledged Twenty bed addiction rehabilitation center. We have reached more
than One Thousand five hundred clients. We also ran branches in Pondicherry,
Chengleput and Nammakal for ADRA Project.
In 2015 Zonta Resource center leased us the present location to continue our services.
CONCERN expanded since then. We Joined hands with Dr. Thiruvikraman, M.B.B.S.,
D.P.M. in psychiatry and started Diya Acute Care Psychiatric care for men and Women
as a Service Provider from September 2019 till October 2023.
Dr. Mohamed Nidhal S., M.D., FNR., PGDHM, Medical Officer and Dr. Sathis
Kumar, M.B.B.S. DPM., DNB., consultant Psychiatrist, joined us from January 2024
and 2024,
COCNERN conducts training programme twice a year on “De-Addiction treatment &
counselling techniques and Social Entrepreneurship.” 18 batches of 25 participants
each have successfully completed. Some of them have started rehabilitation centers on
their own.
CONCERN is recognized as District De-Addiction Center (DDAC) and sponsored by
the “Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment (MSJE)” for the scheme “National
Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR).” We have launched the scheme from
January 2024.

Message from Suresh Babu,
Trustee – Secretary
It is my immense pleasure to share a few words with our esteemed clients, their families,
Sponsors, volunteers, respectful donors and my dear staff.
The menace of addiction to various mood-altering substances is on the rise, today, there
is a growing awareness and recognition of addiction as a disease – a change in attitude
that releases the suffering ones from the fierce moral condemnation.
Substance use disorders are serious problem adversely affecting the social fabric of the
country. Dependence to any substance not only affects the individual's health but also
disrupts their families and the whole society. Regular consumption of various
psychoactive substances leads to dependence of the individual. Therefore, substance use
and dependence needs to be viewed as a psycho-social-medical problem.
It was not easy to meticulously follow our Mission, Vision and Values of CONCERN,
particularly in this field of Addiction Rehabilitation. Unlike other ailments, slavery to
addiction needs empathy, treatment and follow-ups. A long journey.
Personally, I have the distinction of imparting recovery mechanisms to the affected, for
which I am truly grateful to Almighty. Due to vast experience acquired over 30 years,
many changes took place in my approach towards recovery.
It is heartening to see the results, by being a tiny part. Believe you me, there is enormous
amount of target, left to be done. Hopefully with the support from all the related sectors,
CONCERN will grow in recovery stature.
My special thanks to “Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment” (MSJE) for
recognizing CONCERN as District De-Addiction Center (DDAC) for the scheme –
National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR).
Kindly accept my genuine gratitude.
Best wishes and Regards
Suresh Babu
Trustee-Secretary

Letter from Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President, CONCERN Trust
C
CONCERN
Dear friends
We at CONCERN in the field of De-addiction rehabilitation have been contributing to
the affected community and empowered change in many families since 2008. Since
inception we have adopted and followed simple, medical, psychological, holistic and
ethical standards.
The journey was arduous. Many a time the tests and trials were difficult to cross over. It
was more difficult since we didn't deviate or compromise on moral, legal and statutory
obligations. We adopted only ethically prescribed practices of rehabilitation treatment.
It's fulfilling and rewarding to note that CONCERN is rated high in success and in
quality by our clients who are in the path of recovery. We wish to remain We wish to
remain grateful to all who worked in tandem with us to enable CONCERN to touch this
mark.
This Year, we have entered a new venture. We have been recognized as "District De-
Addiction Center (DDAC)" by the "Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
(MJSE)" for the Scheme "Nation Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR)". We
are happy to announce that we have launched the scheme from 1st of January 2024.
Zonta Resource centre, our landlord inducted me as a Trustee on 08.08.2023 to carry
out the services for women empowerment.
I thank the Higher Power who had chosen me in this Mission and I thank all the donors,
volunteers and specially MSJE. My special thanks all my dedicated staff who stand
behind me to walk ahead to reach our Vision.
Regards
Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President
CONCERN Trust
Genesis
CONCERN
CONCERN is an NGO working in the field of addiction since 2008 Bhanu Suresh
babu, M.A., (M.Phil.) in psychology is the president of the Trust. She underwent the
perils of Addiction in her family and found a way out by Divine intervention and
professional help for TTK Hospital.
Suresh Babu, M.B.A., C.A.I.I.B., D.C.A., Co-founder of CONCERN joined and
worked as Honorary Trustee till his tenure in his banking service. Now as a full time
Working trustee and leads the team of CONCERN.
K. Subramani was the Treasurer from February 2008 December 2020. He resigned
from his post due to his personal commitments.
Later Ms. Pavitra Ramachandran, M.Sc., in Bio Medical Genetics joined as the
Treasurer of the CONCERN Trust from 09.01.2021.
CONCERN started in a small Two-bedroom house in Valsaravakam with Ten beds is
now a full-fledged Twenty bed addiction rehabilitation center. We have reached more
than One Thousand five hundred clients. We also ran branches in Pondicherry,
Chengleput and Nammakal for ADRA Project.
In 2015 Zonta Resource center leased us the present location to continue our services.
CONCERN expanded since then. We Joined hands with Dr. Thiruvikraman, M.B.B.S.,
D.P.M. in psychiatry and started Diya Acute Care Psychiatric care for men and Women
as a Service Provider from September 2019 till October 2023.
Dr. Mohamed Nidhal S., M.D., FNR., PGDHM joined us as Medical Officer from the 1st
of January 2024 and from February 2024, Dr. Sathis Kumar, M.B.B.S. DPM., DNB.,
joined us as a consultant Psychiatrist.
COCNERN conducts training programme twice a year on "Social Entrepreneurship with
De-Addiction treatment and counselling techniques". 18 batches of 25 participants each
have successfully completed.
CONCERN is recognized as District De-Addiction Center (DDAC) by the "Ministry
of Social Justice and Empowerment (MSJE)" for the scheme "National Plan for
Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR)". Launched the scheme from 1st of January 2024.

Message from Suresh Babu,
Trustee Secretary
It is my immense pleasure to share a few words with our esteemed clients, their families,
volunteers, respectful donors and my dear staff.
The menace of addiction to various mood-altering substances is on the rise, today, there
is a growing awareness and recognition of addiction as a disease - a change in attitude
that releases the suffering ones from the fierce moral condemnation.
Substance use disorders are serious problem adversely affecting the social fabric of the
country. Dependence to any substance not only affects the individual's health but also
disrupts their families and the whole society. Regular consumption of various
psychoactive substances leads to dependence of the individual. Therefore, substance use
and dependence needs to be viewed as a psycho-social-medical problem.
It was not easy to meticulously follow our Mission, Vision and Values of CONCERN,
particularly in this field of Addiction Rehabilitation. Unlike other ailments, slavery to
addiction needs empathy, treatment and follow-ups. A long journey.
Personally, I have the distinction of imparting recovery mechanisms to the affected, for
which I am truly grateful to Almighty. Due to vast experience acquired over the years,
many changes took place in my approach towards recovery.
It's heartening to see the results, by being a tiny part. Believe you me, there is enormous
amount of target, left to be done. Hopefully with the support from all the related sectors,
CONCERN will grow in recovery stature.
My special thanks to Executives and members of "Ministry of Social Justice and
Empowerment" (MSJE) for recognizing CONCERN as District De-Addiction Center
(DDAC) for the scheme - "National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction"
(NAPDDR).
Kindly accept my genuine gratitude.
Best wishes and Regards
Suresh Babu
Trustee-Secretary

Letter from Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President, CONCERN Trust
Dear friends
We at CONCERN in the field of De-Addiction rehabilitation have been contributing to
the affected community and bringing change in many families since 2008. Since
inception we have adopted and followed simple, medical, psychological, holistic and
ethical standards.
The journey was arduous. Many a time the tests and trials were difficult to cross over. It
was more demanding since we didn't deviate or compromise on moral, legal and
statutory obligations. We adopt only ethically prescribed practices of rehabilitation
treatment.
Our Mission, Vision and Values are altruistic. We stick to them and put it in practical
day to day functioning of CONCERN.
It's fulfilling and rewarding to note that CONCERN is rated high in success and in
quality by our clients who are in the path of recovery. We wish to remain grateful to all
who worked in tandem with us to enable CONCERN to accomplish this reward.
We are happy to share that our “District De-Addiction Center (DDAC)” is performing
very well fulfilling the resolve of “Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
(MJSE)” for the Scheme “Nation Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR)”.
I thank the Higher Power who had chosen me in this Mission and I thank SBI, well-
wishers, donors, volunteers and specially MSJE GOI. My special thanks to all my
dedicated staff who stand behind me to walk ahead to fulfill our Vision, Mission and
Values.
Regards
Bhanu Suresh Babu
Founder President

Genesis
CONCERN is an NGO working in the field of addiction since 2008. Bhanu Suresh babu, M.A.,
(M.Phil.) in psychology is the founder president of the Trust. She underwent the perils of
addiction in her family and found a way out by Divine intervention, professional help of TTK
Hospital led by Dr. Shanthi Ranganathan and Al-Anon fellowship.
Suresh Babu, M.B.A., C.A.I.I.B., D.C.A., Co-founder of CONCERN. He retired from SBI as
Manager. As a Trustee, he manages the policy making, execution and administration. His
exposure to Addiction recovery tools is over 30 years.
Ms. Pavitra Ramachandran, M,Sc., PhD., in Stem cells is the Treasurer of the CONCERN
Trust.
CONCERN started in a small Two-bedroom house in Valasaravakkam with Ten beds is now a
full-fledged Twenty bed addiction rehabilitation center. We have reached more than Two
Thousand clients. We also ran branches in Pondicherry, Chengleput and Nammakal for ADRA
Project.
In 2015 Zonta Resource center leased us the present location to continue our services.

Message from Suresh Babu,
Trustee – Secretary
It is my immense pleasure to share a few words with our esteemed clients, their families,
volunteers, respectful donors and my dear staff.
The menace of addiction to various mood-altering substances is on the rise, today, there
is a growing awareness and recognition of addiction as a disease – a change in attitude
that releases the suffering ones from the fierce moral condemnation.
Substance use disorders are serious problem adversely affecting the social fabric of the
country. Dependence to any substance not only affects the individual's health but also
disrupts their families and the whole society. Regular consumption of various
psychoactive substances leads to compulsive dependence. Therefore, substance use and
dependence needs to be viewed as a psycho-social-medical problem.
It was not easy to meticulously follow our Mission, Vision and Values of CONCERN,
particularly in this field of Addiction Rehabilitation. Unlike other ailments, slavery to
addiction needs empathy, treatment and follow-ups. A long journey.
Personally, I have the distinction of imparting recovery mechanisms to the affected, for
which I am truly grateful to Almighty. Due to vast experience acquired over 30 years,
many evolving changes took place in my approach towards recovery.
It’s heartening to see the results, by being a tiny part. Believe you me, there is enormous
amount of target, left to be done. Hopefully with the support from all the related sectors,
CONCERN will grow in recovery stature.
My special thanks to “Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment” (MSJE) for
recognizing CONCERN as District De-Addiction Center (DDAC) for the scheme –
National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR). And also to State Bank
of India for their regular CSR support and all the donors.
Kindly accept my genuine gratitude.
Best wishes and Regards

Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment (MSJE)
Scheme
“National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction” (NAPDDR)
Substance use disorders are serious problem adversely affecting the social fabric of the country.
Dependence to any substance not only affects the individual's health but also disrupts their families and the
whole society. Regular consumption of various psychoactive substances leads to dependence of the
individual. Therefore, substance use and dependence needs to be viewed as a psycho-social-medical
problem.
2. The Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MOSJ&E) being the nodal Ministry for Drug
Demand Reduction in the country has been working with the objective of creating awareness and educating
people about the ill-effects of alcoholism and substance abuse and for providing a whole range of
community-based services for identification, motivation, counselling, de- addiction, after care and
rehabilitation for Whole Person Recovery (WPR) of substance and alcohol users.
3. The first National Survey on Extant and Pattern of Substance Use in India, 2019 was conducted by
MOSJE through National Drug Dependence Treatment Centre (NDDTC), AIIMS. As per the report,
Alcohol is the most common psychoactive substance used by Indians followed by Cannabis and Opioids.
About 16 Crore persons consume alcohol in the country, 3.1 Crore individuals use cannabis products and
2.26 Crore use opioids. A sizeable number of individuals use sedatives and inhalants.
4. In order to prevent the substance use and dependence in the country, MoSJ&E has formulated and
enacted National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR) (2018- 2025). All the initiatives
under NAPDDR aim towards DRUG DEMAND REDUCTION in the country that can be carried out
through Government of India, State/UT Governments, implementing agencies like PRIS, NGOs, Trusts,
ULBS, Autonomous organizations, Technical Forums, Hospitals, Prison etc. NAPDDR is proposed to be
implemented through both modes of funding as in a central sector and a centrally sponsored scheme with
100% funding from the Centre.
DDAC comprises of
 Integrated Rehabilitation Centre for Addicts (IRCA)
In-Patient free treatment and rehabilitation with staying and food facility. Whole person Recovery is the
main purpose of this facility. The treatment and rehabilitation includes individual counselling, group
therapy, medical assistance, recreation, art therapy, In-house meetings and all related support.
 Out-Reach Drop-in Centre (ODIC)
The substance abusers can make use of this “free facility” by availing the day care Assistance viz. medical
assessment, counselling, first aid, motivation counselling for IRCA, Recreation and relaxation.
 Community based Peer Led Intervention (CPLI)
Our centre CONCERN will reach out to the community by visiting them. Conduct various awareness
programmes to educate the ill effects of substance abuse. Motivate them to form peer groups and lead
them to ODIC and IRCA. The main purpose of this scheme is to concentrate on children, Teens and Youths
for early prevention of substance abuse.The theme is
“Catch them Young”
“All the above facilities are free of cost.”
